questions.question: A rectangular prism has length 5 cm, width 4 cm, and height 3 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a rectangular prism = length × width × height = 5 × 4 × 3 = 60 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has radius 2 inches and height 7 inches. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of cylinder = πr²h = π(2)²(7) = π(4)(7) = 28π cubic inches.

questions.question: A sphere has radius 3 meters. Which expression gives its volume?
A cone has radius 4 cm and height 9 cm. What is its volume in terms of π?
A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 12 square inches and height 5 inches. What is the prism's volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of prism = base area × height = 12 × 5 = 60 cubic inches.

questions.question: A cylinder with radius 3 feet has volume 45π cubic feet. What is its height?
questions.explanation: Volume = πr²h, so 45π = π(3)²h = 9πh. Thus h = 45π ÷ 9π = 5 feet.
